title: Weekly Active Users and Week-over-Week Growth

landmines.name: rows-and-internal-users
landmines.kills: naive/1_rows_and_internal.sql -- COUNT(*) counts event rows (repeat activity + double-fires) instead of COUNT(DISTINCT user_id), and forgetting is_internal=0 leaves staff/QA/test traffic in the active count.

landmines.name: year-reset-week-numbering
landmines.kills: naive/2_yearreset_week.sql -- bucketing by a year-agnostic week label (year || week-of-year) resets the counter at the 2024/2025 boundary and mis-numbers week 53 / week 00, splitting or merging the boundary weeks; the correct bucket is a monotonic Monday-anchored week that never resets.

notes: Weeks are Monday-anchored 7-day buckets built from a literal calendar CTE (the window starts Monday 2024-01-01), giving a MONOTONIC week index that does not reset at the 2024/2025 year boundary and aligns with ISO weeks; each week is labelled by its Monday (week_start). This is the portable substitute for a per-engine ISO-week function. Events map to a week by half-open range (wk_start <= event_ts < wk_end). WAU = COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) with is_internal=0. Every calendar week 2024-01-01..2025-03-31 is reported (LEFT JOIN calendar), so a zero-activity week reads 0. wow_growth = (wau - prev_wau)/prev_wau via LAG over week order, guarded with NULLIF(prev,0): the first week (no predecessor) and any week following a zero-activity week are NULL, not 0 and not an error. orderMatters=true, week_start ascending (a non-null date string, deterministic). naive/2 is the SQLite-dialect form of the year-reset bug (the same failure appears as EXTRACT/to_char/YEARWEEK on other engines).
